name: T3.5-1440p-without-raw-camera-writer
description: T3.2 minus the raw H.264 camera writer. Camera source and PiP overlay remain so the compositor still does its camera-composited work — the only thing removed is the second concurrent H.264 encode session. Splits H.264 engine load differently. If this passes where T3.2 fails, task-4 Path C (drop the raw camera writer at 1440p+, accept post-recording extraction from the composited HLS output) becomes evidence-backed. Note: T2.5 is a simpler version of this test (no camera source or overlay at all); T3.5 keeps the camera in the compositor to isolate the writer from the compositor's camera work.
